- Action model: UP/DOWN moves agent vertically by 6 rows. RIGHT extends the path rightward. Need to test what happens when path reaches a colored block.
- Plan: Grab Blue, move UP to shift Red and Green UP, then see the result. This might help rearrange blocks.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! After grabbing Blue and moving UP:
- Agent stayed at rows 18-23 (didn't move!)
- Red stayed at rows 13-16
- Blue stayed at rows 19-22, cols 42-45 (grabbed)
- Green stayed at rows 31-34
The agent didn't move UP! This is different from earlier when the agent DID move UP. Why?
Looking at the ascii, the agent is at rows 18-23 with a path extending RIGHT to Blue. When I tried to move UP, the agent stayed in place.
Maybe the agent can't move UP because it's already at the top of its reachable range? Or maybe the path being extended RIGHT prevents vertical movement?
Wait, earlier I successfully moved UP when the agent was at rows 30-35 and grabbed Green. The agent moved from 30-35 to 24-29. So vertical movement IS possible when a block is grabbed.
But in this case, the agent is at rows 18-23 and tried to move UP but didn't. Maybe the agent can't move UP from rows 18-23 because it would go to rows 12-17, which might be out of bounds or blocked.
Actually, looking at the ascii, there's a gray path on the left side. The agent might be constrained to move along this path. Let me check if the agent can move DOWN.
Let me try moving DOWN instead.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Agent at rows 18-23 with Blue grabbed. UP didn't move the agent - maybe blocked or at boundary. Need to test DOWN.
Plan: Release Blue first, then try a different approach. Maybe try grabbing Green and moving UP to shift blocks.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# First, release Blue by retracting LEFT
predict(expect_change=True, note='Release Blue by retracting LEFT')
action(['LEFT'] * 20)

seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
print("=== After release Blue ===")
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] in ['R', 'b', 'N'] and n['pixels'] == 16:
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        if min(rows) < 50:
            print(f"  {n['color']}: rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] == 'M' and n['pixels'] == 18:
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        if min(rows) < 50:
            print(f"  Agent: rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}")

# Now try grabbing Green and moving UP
# Agent is at rows 18-23, need to move DOWN x2 to align with Green at rows 31-34
print("\nMoving DOWN x2 to align with Green...")
predict(expect_change=True, note='Move DOWN x2 to align with Green')
action(['DOWN', 'DOWN'])

seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
print("=== After DOWN x2 ===")
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] in ['R', 'b', 'N'] and n['pixels'] == 16:
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        if min(rows) < 50:
            print(f"  {n['color']}: rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] == 'M' and n['pixels'] == 18:
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        if min(rows) < 50:
            print(f"  Agent: rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}")

# Grab Green
print("\nGrabbing Green...")
predict(expect_change=True, note='Grab Green')
action(['RIGHT'] * 5)

seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
print("=== After grab Green ===")
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] in ['R', 'b', 'N'] and n['pixels'] == 16:
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        if min(rows) < 50:
            print(f"  {n['color']}: rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

# Move UP x2 - this should move Red and Blue UP
print("\nMoving UP x2...")
predict(expect_change=True, note='Move UP x2 - expect Red and Blue to move UP')
action(['UP', 'UP'])

seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
print("=== After UP x2 ===")
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] in ['R', 'b', 'N'] and n['pixels'] == 16:
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        if min(rows) < 50:
            print(f"  {n['color']}: rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] == 'M' and n['pixels'] == 18:
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        if min(rows) < 50:
            print(f"  Agent: rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
